name: Hasenpfeffer (Spicy Braised Rabbit)
recipeIngredient: 3 lb Frozen rabbit, thawed and cut
recipeInstructions:
1. Sprinkle rabbit with salt and coat with 1/3 cup flour, shaking off excess.
2. Fry bacon in a Dutch oven over medium heat until crisp; remove bacon and drain on paper towels.
3. Brown several pieces of rabbit in hot bacon fat; remove browned pieces. Repeat with remaining rabbit.
4. Remove all but 2 tablespoons fat from the Dutch oven.
5. Cook and stir shallots and garlic in hot fat for about 4 minutes, until shallots are tender.
6. Stir in wine, 1 cup water, and instant bouillon. Bring to a boil.
7. Stir in jelly, peppercorns, bay leaf, rosemary, and thyme. Return rabbit and bacon to the Dutch oven.
8. Boil briefly, then reduce heat. Cover and simmer until rabbit is tender, about 1 1/2 hours.
9. Remove bay leaf before serving. Place rabbit on a warm platter; keep warm while preparing gravy.
10. Stir lemon juice into the liquid in the Dutch oven.
11. In a covered jar, mix together 3 tablespoons water and 2 tablespoons flour. Squeeze through cheesecloth to incorporate dried thyme leaves.
12. Stir for 1 minute. If the gravy is too thick, add more water until reaching the desired consistency.
13. Serve the gravy with the rabbit.
